mechanism_narrative: Rising real retail sales (RSAFS) indicate strong consumer demand, which drives revenue growth for S&P 500 firms and leads to positive forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If RSAFS trends upward but SPX 20-day returns are not positive in a majority of months, or rank IC over rolling 2-year windows remains below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer income and spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by 0.3 percentage points or more over a 3-month period
failure_condition: If UNRATE triggers this condition but SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of such events over a 2-year window, hypothesis is refuted.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) is elevated while the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) remains low, real interest rates become increasingly negative, driving capital into equities as a yield-seeking alternative and lifting S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the joint signal from low real rates does not produce rank IC above 0.03 over rolling 2-year windows, or if in low-inflation environments the signal direction reverses, hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion, continued employment gains (PAYEMS) alongside stagnating or declining industrial production (INDPRO) indicate capacity constraints and peaking cycle, which historically precede equity drawdowns. This joint signal predicts negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-trading-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS continues to rise while INDPRO growth turns negative over a 3-month period
failure_condition: If after the trigger condition SPX 60-day returns are positive in more than 40% of instances, or if rank IC of composite signal < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP (GDPC1) reflects broad economic expansion, which boosts corporate earnings and supports higher equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: If GDPC1 is positive but SPX 60-day returns are negative in more than 50% of quarters, or if rank IC is below 0.03 over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) above the 1.0% threshold signals commencement of a monetary tightening cycle, which discounts future cash flows more heavily and depresses equity prices over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If after the trigger SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of events, or if rank IC over 2-year rolling windows < 0.03, hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: In a low inflation regime (CPIAUCSL year-on-year change below 3%), an increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) reflects economic strengthening and normalization, which is interpreted positively by equity markets, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by 25 basis points or more over a 2-month period
failure_condition: If in low inflation regime, FEDFUNDS rises but SPX 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of such events, or if the predictive rank IC is below 0.03 over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening construction activity and consumer confidence, which undermines short-term economic sentiment and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST drops by more than 5% month-over-month
failure_condition: If after the housing starts drop SPX 5-day returns are positive in more than 50% of occurrences, or if rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
